events.title: Cupertino Campout
events.address: Creekside Park, 10455 Miller Avenue, Cupertino, CA, 95014, View Map
events.description: The Cupertino Campout is a weekend to remember! Families are invited to pitch a tent at Creekside Park and join us for a night under the stars, filled with recreational activities. This campout is a fantastic way to gather with family, neighbors, and friends or make new ones, all while enjoying all kinds of fun. Pre-registration is required. For more information, please visit the Cupertino Campout webpage. Register

events.title: Free Home Composting Education Workshop
events.address: Cupertino Library, 10800 Torre Avenue, 95014, View Map
events.description: The UC Cooperative Extension Composting Education Program, sponsored by the Recycling and Waste Reduction Commission of Santa Clara County, offers community classes throughout Santa Clara County that teach residents how to build compost piles and worm bins and apply the compost in their landscape. Register in advance at UCCE Composting Education Program registration, or call 408-918-4640. View additional details about these classes on the Home Compost page. Please note: Composting Basics Workshop scheduled for April 11 is canceled due to rain. Time and location change for the September 26 date.

events.description: The UC Cooperative Extension Composting Education Program, sponsored by the Recycling and Waste Reduction Commission of Santa Clara County, offers community classes throughout Santa Clara County that teach residents how to build compost piles and worm bins and apply the compost in their landscape. Register in advance at UCCE Composting Education Program registration, or call 408-918-4640. View additional details about these classes on the Home Compost page. Please note: Composting Basics Workshop scheduled for April 11 is canceled due to rain. Time and location change for the September 26 date.

events.title: Join-A-Ranger: Wildlife Tracking
events.address: McClellan Ranch Preserve, 22221 McClellan Rd, 95014, View Map
events.description: Join a Park Ranger for a fun-filled wildlife tracking class. You'll learn about species identification through their tracks and sign-tracking. Become a nature detective and learn to read the ground like a storybook through the art of tracking. Please wear sturdy walking shoes and long pants that can get dirty. All attendees must register and children must be accompanied by an adult. Register

events.title: Movies in the Park
events.address: Creekside Park, 10455 Miller Avenue, Cupertino, CA, 95014, View Map
events.description: Enjoy popular releases of new, modern, and classic films to enjoy on a projector-screen at Creekside Park. Watch from genres of fantasy, comedy, drama, and action under the stars. For more information, visit the Movies in the Park webpage.

events.title: Middle School Bike Skills
events.address: Cupertino Senior Center, 21251 Stevens Creek Blvd, 95014, View Map
events.description: Middle School Bike Skills workshops prepare rising 6th, 7th, and 8th graders to bike to middle school and beyond. The original course teaches students and their parent or guardian safe biking fundamentals. New this year is a sequel which teaches students how to adapt those skills to stay safe while riding an e-bike and a third course teaching students the basics of maintaining and doing simple repairs on their own bikes. MSBS 101 - Middle School Bike Workshop MSBS 102 - e-Bike Workshop MSBS 103 - Bike Maintenance and Repair Workshop This Middle School Bike Skills program is brought to you by Cupertino Safe Routes to School in partnership with Wheel Kids. Registration is required. Links and more information can be found at www.cupertino.gov/saferoutes/msbs

events.title: Cupertino Fall Bike Fest
events.address: Cupertino Civic Plaza, 10300 Torre Ave, 95014, View Map
events.description: Strap on a helmet and bike to Civic Center Plaza to start the fall with some bike-themed fun! The event will feature bike rides, on-bike and off-bike games, arts and crafts, food and beverages for sale, and much more! Check out the Bike Fest website for the schedule, list of events, and registration form: www.cupertino.gov/bikefest

events.title: Na Leo Mokupuni in Cupertino
events.address: Community Hall, 10350 Torre Ave., 95014, View Map
events.description: Na Leo Mokupuni in Cupertino – meaning island sounds in Cupertino Experience the rhythms, songs, and dances of the islands! Na Leo Mokupuni brings together performers to celebrate the vibrant traditions of island music and dance—right here in Cupertino. Preregistration is recommended. Limited tickets available at the door, while supplies last. Register

events.title: Pooch Plunge
events.address: Blackberry Farm Park, 21979 San Fernando Avenue, Cupertino, CA, 95014, View Map
events.description: Let your pup take the plunge during a dog-only swim in the Blackberry Farm pools. It’s a paws-itively fun way for your pooch to enjoy the pools before they open for summer swimming—tail wags are highly encouraged. Swim Sessions (45 minutes of tail-wagging fun) Block 1 - 9 to 9:45 a.m. Block 2 - 10 to 10:45 a.m. Block 3 - 11 to 11:45 a.m. Block 4 - Noon to 12:45 p.m. Register Registration opens in February for the spring event and in June for the fall event. For additional information, visit the Pooch Plunge webpage.

events.title: Pizza and Politics
events.address: Cupertino Community Hall, 10350 Torre Ave, Cupertino, CA, 95014, View Map
events.description: Pizza and Politics is an award-winning, youth-led biannual pre-election political forum coordinated by youth for youth. The event encourages youth and teen civic engagement, makes politics more accessible, and provides young people with the opportunity to connect directly with politicians. For more information, please visit the Pizza and Politics event page.

events.title: Hidden Treasures
events.address: Cupertino Senior Center, 21251 Stevens Creek Boulevard, Cupertino, CA, 95014, View Map
events.description: Join us at the Senior Center for Hidden Treasures! With hundreds of items to choose from, this event is the perfect opportunity to discover unique antiques, artwork, handknits, toys, glassware, housewares, jewelry, and more. For more information, please visit the Hidden Treasures event page.

events.title: Monster Mash
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, Cupertino, CA, 95014, View Map
events.description: Get ready for a fang-tastic good time at Cupertino Parks and Recreation’s annual Monster Mash Halloween event at the Quinlan Community Center. Put on your favorite costume and enjoy an evening of games, trick-or-treating, spooky crafts, and pumpkin decorating. It’s a fun-filled night of Halloween activities for the whole family. Register For more information, please visit the Monster Mash event page.

events.title: 2026 Cupertino Community Service Awards
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 N. Stelling Road, 95014, View Map
events.description: The third annual Cupertino Community Service Awards will take place on Wednesday, October 28. This ceremony will recognize the 2026 CREST Awards (Cupertino Recognizes Extra Steps Taken), and Commissioners who served on a City Commission. More details and registration information for the October 28 event will be available soon. For more information and to submit a nomination for the CREST Awards, visit cupertino.gov/crest.

events.title: Tree Lighting
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, Cupertino, CA, 95014, View Map
events.description: Kick off the holiday season at the City’s annual Community Tree Lighting! Enjoy the moment the tree is illuminated with hundreds of twinkling lights, live music from a local choir, and festive holiday cheer. After the tree lighting, families can visit Santa and take photos. For more information, please visit the Tree Lighting event page.

events.title: Breakfast with Santa
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, 95014, View Map
events.description: You and your family are invited to a festive breakfast celebration with Santa! Enjoy delicious North Pole pancakes prepared by Santa’s elves, take photos with Santa, and explore a holiday workshop filled with crafts and activities for children. Three seatings: 8:30 a.m., 9:30 a.m., 10:30 a.m. Register Registration opens in late July. For more information, please visit the Breakfast with Santa event page.

events.title: Signing Santa
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, 95014, View Map
events.description: Deaf and hard of hearing children, along with their families, are invited to spend time with a Santa who understands sign language! Santa only communicates using sign language but an interpreter will be on-site to help translate for friends and family. Register Registration opens in late July. For more information, please visit the Signing Santa event page.

events.title: The Employee Dharma Handbook
events.description: As an important rocket launch approaches, Val, an HR Executive at an aerospace company in Silicon Valley, suspects that a young engineer was overlooked for a promotion. Leela, a first generation Indian-American, doesn’t really want to talk about it, but Val suspects sexism is the cause. What they don’t expect to uncover are simmering tensions of ancient origins among the company’s Indian immigrant employees…sorry, Valued Team Members. Soon Val is navigating a funhouse of identity, nepotism, and desire as she tries to understand the culture and biases of Leela’s managers. When push comes to shove, Val will need to decide what her duty is to Leela, to the company, and to herself.

When push comes to shove, Val will need to decide what her duty is to Leela, to the company, and to herself.

events.title: Dracula
events.venue: Mountain View Center for the Performing Arts
events.description: Sink your teeth into the ultimate Halloween thrill. Steven Dietz’s adaptation of the timeless classic seduces with elegance and suspense, turning the legendary vampire tale into a high-octane theatrical ride. Bold, chilling, and wickedly smart, the story draws you into a world where reason clashes with superstition and love becomes dangerously intoxicating. This Dracula keeps you leaning forward—never quite sure who holds the power, or what darkness waits just out of sight.

events.title: CrossFit Games
events.address: 525 W Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113
events.description: Parking for the 2026 CrossFit Games Attending Multiple Days? The 3-Day Parking Pass is the recommended option for guests attending more than one day of the CrossFit Games. Enjoy the convenience of a single parking purchase for the entire weekend, guaranteed access to the ABC Lot, and the flexibility to come and go as needed throughout the event. 3-Day Parking Pass (ABC Lot) Guaranteed parking access in the ABC Lot all weekend Unlimited in-and-out privileges throughout the event One parking credential valid for all three event days No need to purchase parking separately each day Exclusive access to the ABC Lot Pricing Advance Purchase: $150 + applicable fees Event-Day Rate (if available): $180 Please note: ABC Lot inventory is limited and may sell out before the event. Advance purchase is strongly recommended. Purchase All 3 Days VIP Parking Package (includes In and Out Privileges in the A-B-C Main Lot) Attending a Single Day? Single-day parking is available in the Delmas Lots. Single-Day Parking Pass (Delmas Lots) Valid only on the selected event day Parking access from 8 AM to 7 PM No in-and-out privileges Re-entry requires a new parking purchase Pricing Advance Purchase: $45 + applicable fees Event-Day Rate: $50 Purchase General Event Parking for Friday, 7/24 Purchase General Event Parking for Saturday, 7/25 Purchase General Event Parking for Sunday 7/26 Why Purchase Parking in Advance? Parking inventory for the CrossFit Games weekend is limited. Purchasing parking before arriving is the easiest way to ensure a smooth and stress-free event experience. Benefits of purchasing in advance: Secure your parking before lots reach capacity Avoid searching for available parking on event day Speed up your arrival experience Guarantee access to your selected parking option Guests attending multiple days are encouraged to purchase the 3-Day Parking Pass early, as ABC Lot inventory is limited. More Info
